moduleDescription: This module implements a small wrapper for some needed Win API procedures, so that the Nim compiler does not depend on the huge Windows module.

entries.name: WINBOOL
entries.description: WINBOOL uses opposite convention as posix, !=0 meaning success.

entries.name: SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES
entries.code:
SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES = object
  nLength*: int32
  lpSecurityDescriptor*: pointer
  bInheritHandle*: WINBOOL

entries.name: STARTUPINFO
entries.code:
STARTUPINFO = object
  cb*: int32
  lpReserved*: cstring
  lpDesktop*: cstring
  lpTitle*: cstring
  dwX*: int32
  dwY*: int32
  dwXSize*: int32
  dwYSize*: int32
  dwXCountChars*: int32
  dwYCountChars*: int32
  dwFillAttribute*: int32
  dwFlags*: int32
  wShowWindow*: int16
  cbReserved2*: int16
  lpReserved2*: pointer
  hStdInput*: Handle
  hStdOutput*: Handle
  hStdError*: Handle

entries.name: PROCESS_INFORMATION
entries.code:
PROCESS_INFORMATION = object
  hProcess*: Handle
  hThread*: Handle
  dwProcessId*: int32
  dwThreadId*: int32

entries.name: FILETIME
entries.code:
FILETIME = object
  dwLowDateTime*: DWORD
  dwHighDateTime*: DWORD
entries.description: CANNOT BE int64 BECAUSE OF ALIGNMENT

entries.name: BY_HANDLE_FILE_INFORMATION
entries.code:
BY_HANDLE_FILE_INFORMATION = object
  dwFileAttributes*: DWORD
  ftCreationTime*: FILETIME
  ftLastAccessTime*: FILETIME
  ftLastWriteTime*: FILETIME
  dwVolumeSerialNumber*: DWORD
  nFileSizeHigh*: DWORD
  nFileSizeLow*: DWORD
  nNumberOfLinks*: DWORD
  nFileIndexHigh*: DWORD
  nFileIndexLow*: DWORD

entries.name: OSVERSIONINFO
entries.code:
OSVERSIONINFO = object
  dwOSVersionInfoSize*: DWORD
  dwMajorVersion*: DWORD
  dwMinorVersion*: DWORD
  dwBuildNumber*: DWORD
  dwPlatformId*: DWORD
  szCSDVersion*: array[0 .. 127, WinChar]

entries.name: Protoent
entries.code:
Protoent = object
  p_name*: cstring
  p_aliases*: cstringArray
  p_proto*: cshort

entries.name: isSuccess
entries.code: proc isSuccess(a: WINBOOL): bool {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns true if a != 0. Windows uses a different convention than POSIX, where a == 0 is commonly used on success.

entries.name: SockAddr
entries.code:
SockAddr {.importc: "SOCKADDR", header: "winsock2.h".} = object
  sa_family*: uint16
  sa_data*: array[0 .. 13, char]

entries.name: InAddr
entries.code:
InAddr {.importc: "IN_ADDR", header: "winsock2.h", union.} = object
  s_addr*: uint32

entries.name: Sockaddr_in
entries.code:
Sockaddr_in {.importc: "SOCKADDR_IN", header: "winsock2.h".} = object
  sin_family*: uint16
  sin_port*: uint16
  sin_addr*: InAddr
  sin_zero*: array[0 .. 7, char]

entries.name: In6_addr
entries.code:
In6_addr {.importc: "IN6_ADDR", header: "winsock2.h".} = object
  bytes* {.importc: "u.Byte".}: array[0 .. 15, char]

entries.name: Sockaddr_in6
entries.code:
Sockaddr_in6 {.importc: "SOCKADDR_IN6", header: "ws2tcpip.h".} = object
  sin6_family*: uint16
  sin6_port*: uint16
  sin6_flowinfo*: int32
  sin6_addr*: In6_addr
  sin6_scope_id*: int32

entries.name: Servent
entries.code:
Servent = object
  s_name*: cstring
  s_aliases*: cstringArray
  when defined(cpu64):
    s_proto*: cstring
    s_port*: int16
  else:
    s_port*: int16
    s_proto*: cstring

entries.name: Hostent
entries.code:
Hostent = object
  h_name*: cstring
  h_aliases*: cstringArray
  h_addrtype*: int16
  h_length*: int16
  h_addr_list*: cstringArray

entries.name: TFdSet
entries.code:
TFdSet = object
  fd_count*: cint
  fd_array*: array[0 .. 64 - 1, SocketHandle]

entries.name: AddrInfo
entries.code:
AddrInfo {.importc: "ADDRINFOA", header: "ws2tcpip.h".} = object
  ai_flags*: cint            ## Input flags.
  ai_family*: cint           ## Address family of socket.
  ai_socktype*: cint         ## Socket type.
  ai_protocol*: cint         ## Protocol of socket.
  ai_addrlen*: csize_t       ## Length of socket address.
  ai_canonname*: cstring     ## Canonical name of service location.
  ai_addr*: ptr SockAddr     ## Socket address of socket.
  ai_next*: ptr AddrInfo     ## Pointer to next in list.

entries.name: Timeval
entries.code:
Timeval {.importc: "struct timeval", header: "<time.h>".} = object
  tv_sec*, tv_usec*: int32
proc `==`(x, y: SocketHandle): bool {.borrow, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
